title: Logical error: 'File format can't be empty for hive style partitioning'
description:
### Describe the bug
Easy to reproduce. Shouldn't the error not be a logical one?
### How to reproduce
Run:
```sql
CREATE TABLE t0 (c0 Int) ENGINE = S3(s3, partition_strategy = 'hive') PARTITION BY (c0);
```
### Error message and/or stacktrace
Stack trace:
```
<Fatal> : Logical error: 'File format can't be empty for hive style partitioning'.
```

bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE t1 (c0 INT, c1 TEXT, c2 DOUBLE PRECISION) WITH (OIDS=FALSE);
CREATE TABLE t2 (c0 INT, c1 TEXT, c2 TIMESTAMP) WITH (OIDS=FALSE);
INSERT INTO t1 (c0, c1, c2) VALUES (1, 'test', 1.5), (2, NULL, 2.5);
INSERT INTO t2 (c0, c1, c2) SELECT c0, c1, NOW() FROM t1 WHERE c0 > 0;
SELECT c0, COUNT(*) FROM t2 GROUP BY c0 HAVING COUNT(*) > 1;
key_bug_features: File format can't be empty for hive partitioning
